description_long: This is a Corinthian-style ancient Greek helmet made of aged, patinated bronze, featuring a prominent crest holder running from the front to the back. The helmet has a full-face design with narrow eye slits and a pronounced nose guard, providing substantial facial protection. The sides extend downward to cover the cheeks, with rounded cheek guards that curve inward. Decorative elements include a raised band across the brow, adorned with a subtle emblem or relief, and circular medallions at the base of the crest holder on each side. The surface is mottled with greenish and brownish oxidation, giving it an authentic, weathered appearance. The crest holder is ribbed and metallic, suggesting it once supported a plume or horsehair crest.
description: A weathered bronze Corinthian helmet with a full-face design, cheek guards, and a ribbed crest holder, featuring patina and subtle decorative details.
description_view_0: Front view shows the helmet's eye slits, nose guard, and brow band with a faint emblem.
description_view_1: Right side view highlights the ribbed crest holder, circular medallion, and cheek guard profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the smooth, rounded back and the full length of the crest holder.
description_view_3: Left side view mirrors the right, showing the crest holder, medallion, and cheek guard.
